summary: The agent concludes that curingtage of the sinus is appropriate due to its role in removing infected tissue and preventing recurrent infections, even though specific documentation about dental sinuses is limited. This action aligns with core endodontic principles emphasizing effective debridement when dealing with an abscessed, nonvital pulp.
